answers.where: Mine Cards Mining screen: fixed top miner stage plus lower activity board.
answers.action: Watch Mining run, read active Surface Stone, inspect locked Copper Vein, then work toward Copper Pickaxe.
answers.response: Runtime now uses generated compact chip/card/button/nav slice9 assets instead of procedural rectangles; framebuffer proof shows the assets render and scale.
answers.reward: Stone, XP, coins, geode feedback, progress bar, and pickaxe costs are visible.
answers.game_look: Generated voxel/RPG UI art is now present across the lower board, not only in the stage and icons.

issues.text: Compact rows and nav tabs are source-derived and cleanly cut, but their ornate corners/green strips dominate the lower board at gameplay sizes.
The lower board now has too many competing generated frames; the active row, future chips, node rows, upgrade card, and nav tabs all fight for attention.
Text remains readable by zoom audit, but dense decorative controls make scanning slower than the Melvor-like reference direction.

problem: Extraction/cropping is fixed, but the compact generated art is too visually heavy when applied wholesale to the lower board; this is an art/application mismatch, not a chroma-key failure.
next: Keep the saved/cut compact sheet as source evidence. Create a calmer compact runtime variant: use generated art for selected/primary states, derive simpler idle rows/tabs from the same source or generate a low-ornament compact sheet, then recapture landscape/portrait and rerun product gate.
